findings.message: devDependency esbuild is not pinned to an exact version; supply-chain updates can pull unvetted code into the build
devDependency @sinclair/typebox is not pinned to an exact version
devDependency nanoid is not pinned to an exact version
Vague quantifier 'accordingly' — replace with a concrete decision rule referencing the examples already present
Vague quantifier 'reasonable' — reader must infer what makes an approach reasonable
Vague quantifier 'obvious' — the choice may not be obvious to the agent in all contexts
Vague quantifier 'too many' — the numeric limit is already defined one line earlier but not referenced

Vague quantifier 'accordingly' — replace with a concrete decision rule referencing the examples already present — Line 31: 'Adjust accordingly: `1-2` for a small fix' — 'accordingly' provides no actionable criterion
Vague quantifier 'reasonable' — reader must infer what makes an approach reasonable — Line 152: 'Questions about the approach when only one is reasonable' — 'reasonable' is undefined
Vague quantifier 'obvious' — the choice may not be obvious to the agent in all contexts — Line 152: 'Respond with the obvious choice' — 'obvious' is subjective and reader-dependent

Vague quantifier 'too many' — the numeric limit is already defined one line earlier but not referenced — Line 252: 'Don't launch too many sessions in parallel' while maxSessions default (5) is stated at line 249

devDependency esbuild is not pinned to an exact version; supply-chain updates can pull unvetted code into the build — "esbuild": "^0.27.3" — caret range allows automatic minor/patch upgrades during npm install
devDependency @sinclair/typebox is not pinned to an exact version — "@sinclair/typebox": "^0.34.48" — caret range allows automatic minor/patch upgrades during npm install
devDependency nanoid is not pinned to an exact version — "nanoid": "^3.3.7" — caret range allows automatic minor/patch upgrades during npm install
